definition: The blocker is a NULL-return allocation-failure branch; context-sensitive coverage records the allocation sub-path as new coverage per call context, steering the corpus toward the heap or input state that makes the allocator return NULL rather than staying on the fast non-NULL path.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook gives every call path through png_handle_sCAL and png_read_buffer its own edge-map bucket, so the allocation-failure sub-state traversal registers as new coverage and the corpus is steered toward the heap state that makes the buffer return NULL; bare-edge naive never rewards this sub-path.
signature.one_line: context-indexed edges reward allocation-failure sub-path through sCAL handler naive's flat map ignores

analysis.summary_one_line: A PNG with an sCAL chunk whose length+1 allocation via png_read_buffer fails, returning NULL and taking the out-of-memory true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed PNG (IHDR present, no prior IDAT, no duplicate sCAL, sCAL length >= 4) that reaches png_read_buffer(png_ptr, length+1, ...) in OSS_FUZZ_png_handle_sCAL under a heap/allocation state where the buffer allocation returns NULL, so the blocked true branch at L2415 is taken. The feature is not a specific literal byte gate but reaching the sCAL handler with an allocation-failing program state.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves the branch in all 10 trials (resolved=10/10, avg hitcount 14, blocked side taken T=11 in the per-role overlay). Its context-sensitive coverage records the sCAL edge under distinct call-context indices, so reaching png_handle_sCAL along varied call paths (and the allocation sub-paths inside png_read_buffer) registers as new coverage and is retained, steering the corpus toward the input/heap state that makes the allocation fail.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks the branch in 9/10 trials (resolved=1/10, blocked side L2415 T=0 F=12). With plain edge coverage the bare sCAL edge is counted once regardless of call context, so once the false (buffer non-NULL) side is hit, traversing png_handle_sCAL again yields no new edge-bucket signal and the campaign is never rewarded for exploring the allocation-failure sub-state.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx installs a CtxHook that folds a running call-context hash into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is a new coverage entry per call path. This (call-context, edge) granularity rewards reaching png_read_buffer / png_handle_sCAL via more contexts, driving exploration into the rarely-taken out-of-memory branch. naive's plain MaxMapFeedback over bare edges gives no such gradient. The divergence is attributable to the context-sensitive coverage technique, the single difference between the two fuzzers.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true branch of 'if (buffer == NULL)' in the sCAL handler, where buffer is the result of png_read_buffer(png_ptr, length+1, ...).
The blocked side is the true branch of the buffer == NULL test.
naive_ctx resolves the branch in every trial while naive almost always blocks it, with a large probability divergence.
The loser naive resolves the branch in only 1 of 10 trials.
In per-role coverage the winner takes the true (out-of-memory) side at L2415 while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L2415  T=11 F=1  T=0 F=12  if (buffer == NULL)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The winner's resolving path goes to the out-of-memory error block (W-only lines 2416-2420).
naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the call context into the edge-map index, making the same edge a distinct coverage slot per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: For naive_ctx a new coverage bucket is a new (call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

evidence_trail.claim: naive uses plain per-edge hit-count bucketing with no context indexing.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add (LibAFL `MaxMapFeedback` over the edge map).

evidence_trail.claim: The buffer is allocated from length+1 bytes, the size whose allocation must fail to return NULL.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with an sCAL-like allocation-failure branch reached through only a single call context, where naive_ctx and naive resolve the out-of-memory true branch at statistically indistinguishable rates, would refute the claim that context-sensitive coverage is what drives the win.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact program-state that makes png_read_buffer return NULL is inferred from the source path rather than directly observed in seed bytes, since the byte-diff offsets (e.g. IHDR/length fields) only weakly tie to the allocation-failure condition.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook re-indexes the png_read_buffer edge per call path, so large-length zTXt inputs that force a NULL allocation earn new (call-context, edge) coverage that bare-edge naive's saturated map never rewards.
signature.one_line: Context indexing keeps large-length zTXt seeds alive toward NULL return

analysis.summary_one_line: A zTXt chunk with a length large enough that png_read_buffer fails to allocate, returning NULL into the buffer==NULL true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A zTXt chunk whose declared length field is large enough that the call buffer = png_read_buffer(png_ptr, length, 2) cannot satisfy the allocation and returns NULL, so the (buffer == NULL) condition at pngrutil.c:2634 evaluates true and control enters the out-of-memory bailout at 2635-2639.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves the branch in all 10 trials (resolved=10/10, hitcount on branch=8) and takes the true side 16 times (L2634 T=16 F=0 for W), i.e. it repeatedly drives png_read_buffer down the NULL-return path; the CtxHook re-indexes each edge by call context so the png_read_buffer / handle_zTXt edges count as fresh coverage under each distinct chunk-processing call path, rewarding the rare large-length zTXt inputs that exhaust the read buffer.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks the branch in 9 of 10 trials (resolved=1/10, blocked=9) and never takes the true side (L2634 T=0 F=35 for L) — its bare edge-bucket feedback marks the png_read_buffer edge covered after the first ordinary zTXt and stops rewarding inputs that push length high enough to force a NULL allocation, so it stays on the false branch and proceeds into the keyword-parsing tail (L2641, L2647-2708 hit only in L).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds a running call-context hash into the edge-map index (HookableInProcessExecutor), so the same png_read_buffer edge is recorded at different map slots per call path; a new (call-context, edge) pair is novel coverage that keeps the large-length / OOM zTXt inputs alive in the corpus, whereas plain naive's MaxMapFeedback over the bare edge map saturates the edge and abandons that direction. This is a coverage-granularity (feedback-dimension) advantage, not comparison solving.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking condition is buffer == NULL, whose buffer comes from png_read_buffer with the chunk length.
The true (winning) side at L2634 is the globally blocked side and leads to the out-of-memory bailout.
naive_ctx wins decisively over naive with ctx_coverage as the single-technique delta.
The winner resolves the branch in all trials while the loser blocks it.
The loser blocks the branch in 9 of 10 trials and almost never resolves it.
In W the branch takes the true side; in L it takes only the false side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L2634  T=16 F=0  T=0 F=35  if (buffer == NULL)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The winner satisfies the allocation-failure path; the out-of-memory bailout lines 2635-2639 are hit only in W.
The loser stays on the false branch and proceeds into the keyword-parsing/CRC tail that only L exercises.
naive_ctx's mechanism re-indexes each edge by call context, making a context+edge pair count as new coverage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

evidence_trail.claim: Plain naive uses MaxMapFeedback over the bare edge map, with no I2S or CMP introspection.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add (LibAFL `MaxMapFeedback` over the edge map).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a synthetic zTXt harness sweeping chunk length showed plain naive resolving the buffer==NULL true branch as often as naive_ctx (no separation), the context-sensitive-coverage attribution would be refuted.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: No seed-byte offset directly shows the zTXt length field driving the allocation past the cap; the OOM trigger is inferred from source plus the W-only out-of-memory lines rather than a byte-diff at the length offset.
